commentary: Eleven sessions this week versus twelve last week, which is close enough that nobody's keeping score — but the shape of the week is what's interesting, because you traded last week's five pool sessions for four, dropped a swim day, and then closed the whole thing out with a 13.1k at 159 bpm, which is running harder than last week's run club at 151 bpm over the exact same distance. That Naremburn effort was not a casual Saturday jog, babe. The double-up days on Tuesday and Wednesday — swim then weights, swim then weights — are becoming a signature move, and the Prince Alfred emoji appearing four times in a row tells its own little story about where your loyalty lies. The one thing worth watching is that recovery walk on Thursday morning sandwiched between two weight sessions; the body was clearly asking for something gentler, and the fact that you listened is actually the smart, unsexy, grown-up thing to do.
